items.subTitle: Attributes
items.description: Understand attributes like id, class, name, value, for element identification.

items.subTitle: DOM (Document Object Model)
items.description: Know how the DOM represents the webpage structure and how automation tools interact with it.

items.subTitle: Selectors
items.description: Use CSS selectors to locate elements, such as #id, .class, input[type='text'].

items.subTitle: Element Visibility
items.description: Understand visibility attributes like display: none and visibility: hidden.

items.subTitle: Events
items.description: Handle user events like click, hover, etc.

items.subTitle: AJAX
items.description: Handle dynamically loaded content and API calls.

items.subTitle: CSS Selectors
items.description: Preferred for simpler element identification (e.g., input[type='text']).

items.subTitle: Inspect Elements
items.description: Use tools like Chrome DevTools to inspect HTML, identify selectors, and debug.

items.subTitle: Network Monitoring
items.description: Monitor network requests for API testing and ensure correct responses.

items.subTitle: Waits
items.description: Use explicit or implicit waits to handle elements that load dynamically.

items.subTitle: API Testing
items.description: Understand how to interact with APIs using methods like GET, POST for backend testing.

items.subTitle: Cross-Browser Testing
items.description: Ensure tests work across different browsers due to potential rendering differences.
